originalTitle: New outbreak of Ebola kills 65 in eastern DR Congo
title: Helping Friends Stay Healthy in Congo
content:
A Little Germ Making Friends Sick

Hello, super friends! We have a story from a big country in Africa called DR Congo. It's a place with beautiful forests and rivers. Sometimes, tiny little germs can make people sick. You can't see these germs with your eyes, just like you can't see the wind. But they are there, and they can make us feel unwell. Right now, a special kind of germ is making some people in DR Congo feel very, very sick. This germ has a tricky name: Ebola. Say it with me: E-bo-la. It's making some grown-ups and children unwell in the eastern part of DR Congo. It's like getting a bad cold or flu, but much stronger. When people get Ebola, they feel very tired and can have a fever. Our friends in DR Congo need our help and our good thoughts. Many grown-ups are working very hard to help them get better. They want to stop this germ from making more people sick.

What is Ebola?

Ebola is a germ, a bit like the flu germ, but it's very special and can be dangerous. It lives in some animals, like certain bats. When it gets into people, it can make them very ill. It's not something you catch by just walking past someone. It spreads when you touch someone who is very sick with it, or touch their blood or other body fluids. **Did you know?** Germs are tiny living things! Some germs are good for us, like the ones that help our tummy digest food. But some germs can make us sick. Ebola is one of those germs that makes people very sick. It can make people feel very hot (have a fever) and tired. It can also make their tummy hurt. This is why doctors and nurses need to be very careful. But don't worry! Many smart doctors and nurses know what to do. They are learning more about Ebola every day to keep everyone safe.

Where is This Happening?

This is happening in a country called the Democratic Republic of Congo. It's often just called DR Congo. It is a very big country in Central Africa. Think of a map of Africa. DR Congo is right in the middle, a very important part of our continent. The germ is making people sick in the eastern part of DR Congo. This part of the country has lots of lovely towns and villages. People there live, play, and go to school just like you. There have been about 246 people who have felt sick because of this germ. Sadly, some people have become so sick that they couldn't get better. About 65 people have died. This is very sad news, and it means we need to help even more. But remember, many people are working day and night to stop this germ from spreading. They are trying to keep families safe in DR Congo.

Who is Helping Our Friends?

Lots of very brave and clever people are helping our friends in DR Congo. There are doctors and nurses who wear special clothes to keep themselves safe while they help sick people. They are like superheroes in their protective gear! There is also a very important group called Africa's top health agency. This group helps all the countries in Africa work together to stay healthy. They are sending helpers, medicine, and special tools to DR Congo. Their job is to make sure everyone knows how to stop the germ and how to help people who are sick. **Did you know?** There are amazing scientists who study germs! They look at germs through very powerful microscopes to understand them better. They are like detectives, trying to figure out how germs work and how to stop them. These helpers are working very, very hard. They want to make sure everyone is safe and healthy. They are showing great kindness and bravery to help people who are unwell.

How Do We Stop This Germ?

Stopping the Ebola germ is very important. It's like building a strong wall to keep the germ away. One big way to stop it is to be very careful about touching things. If someone is sick with Ebola, other people need to be careful not to touch them directly. This is why doctors wear special gloves and masks. When someone is sick, they get special care in a safe place. This helps them get better and stops the germ from spreading to others. Another very important thing is to make sure that people who have died from Ebola are buried very safely and carefully. This is because the germ can still be there even after someone has passed away. Special teams help families do this in a way that keeps everyone else safe. **Did you know?** Sometimes, scientists make special medicines called vaccines. A vaccine can teach your body how to fight a germ before you even get sick! They are also trying to make vaccines for Ebola to help protect people in the future.

Why Washing Hands is So Important!

This is something we can all do to help! Washing our hands is super important for fighting germs. It's like washing away tiny, invisible monsters from our hands. When we wash our hands with soap and water, we get rid of many germs. This stops them from getting into our bodies and making us sick. It also stops us from passing germs to our friends and family. Think about it: after you play outside, before you eat, or after you use the toilet, what should you do? Wash your hands! Use lots of soap and rub them for a long time. Sing a song while you wash, like "Happy Birthday" twice. That's how long you should wash! **Did you know?** Your hands have lots of tiny creases and lines where germs can hide. Soap and water get into all those places! Even though Ebola is a serious germ, washing hands helps fight many other germs too, like those that cause colds. So, let's all be super hand washers! It's an easy way to stay healthy and protect others.

Brave Helpers Are Working Hard

All over DR Congo, and from other parts of Africa and the world, brave helpers are there. They are working very hard, every single day. They are teaching people about the germ and how to stay safe. They are giving special care to those who are sick. They are comforting families who are sad. These helpers are true heroes. They are helping to bring hope to the people in DR Congo. They believe that we can stop this germ. And they are doing everything they can to make sure that happens.

Let's Keep Our Friends Safe

So, remember, even though there's a serious germ called Ebola making some people sick in DR Congo, there's also lots of good happening. Brave doctors, nurses, and health workers are on the job. They are working together to fight the germ and keep everyone safe. We can help by thinking good thoughts for our friends in DR Congo. And we can always remember to wash our hands properly, every single day! This helps us stay healthy and strong, and it shows we care about stopping germs everywhere. Together, we can help Africa stay healthy and strong.
summary: Brave doctors and helpers are working hard in DR Congo to stop a germ called Ebola from making people sick and to help those who are unwell.
whyMatters: It's important because many people are working together to keep everyone healthy and safe from germs like Ebola in Africa, showing we care about each other.

originalTitle: Former Nigerian minister sentenced to 75 years in rare corruption verdict
title: Fair Play for Africa: A Big Lesson About Helping Everyone!
content:
Hello, Super Friends!

Hello, my amazing friends across Africa! Your kid-friendly news reporter is here with another important story. We travel all over our beautiful continent to bring you news that matters. Today, we have a story from a big country called Nigeria. It's about being fair and making sure everyone gets what they need. So, let's learn together!

A Big Story from Nigeria

Guess what? There was a man in Nigeria who used to have a very important job. His name is Saleh Mamman. He used to be in charge of 'Power' for the whole country. Think about it – all the lights, the TVs, charging your parent's phone, the computers at school! That's a huge job, right? But something went wrong. This man was found to have done some things that were not fair. The grown-ups called it 'corruption'. Don't worry, we will learn what that big word means soon! It's important for all of us to understand.

Who is a Minister, Anyway?

First, let's talk about what a 'Minister' is. Imagine your school has many different clubs, like a sports club, a reading club, or a music club. Each club might have a leader or a captain. This leader helps the club run well. In a country, it's kind of similar! A country has many important jobs. There's someone who helps make sure everyone has good schools (that's an Education Minister). Someone makes sure people are healthy (a Health Minister). And Mr. Saleh Mamman was the 'Power Minister'. This means he was like the main leader for making sure everyone had electricity. **Did you know?** Being a minister is a very big responsibility! You have to think about all the people in your country and how to help them.

What is Power and Why Do We Need It?

Power, or electricity, is super important for all of us. Think about when the lights go out at home! It gets dark, your TV stops working, and you can't charge your tablet or phone to play games or do homework. Electricity helps us do so many things! It lights up our homes and schools. It keeps our food cold in refrigerators. It powers the machines that help doctors in hospitals. It even helps factories make the toys you play with and the clothes you wear. Without good electricity, life can be very hard. That's why the Power Minister's job is so, so important. He had to make sure power was working well for all the boys and girls and grown-ups in Nigeria.

The Problem: Something Called Corruption

Now, let's talk about that big word: corruption. It might sound scary, but it just means someone is not being honest or fair, especially with money or power. Imagine you and your friend have some sweets to share. But your friend secretly takes more than their fair share, or even takes some of your sweets without asking. That's not fair, right? That's a little bit like what corruption is, but with much bigger things, like money for the whole country. When a person in an important job uses the country's money or power for themselves, instead of helping everyone, that's corruption. It's like taking the money meant to build a new school or fix the electricity and keeping it for yourself or giving it to your friends who didn't earn it. **Did you know?** When there is corruption, it means there might not be enough money to build good roads, safe hospitals, or even new schools for children like you!

What Happened in Court?

When someone does something wrong, especially when they have an important job, there are special places called courts. In a court, there are judges who are like very wise teachers. They listen to all the facts and decide if someone has broken the rules or done something bad. Mr. Saleh Mamman went to court. The judges and other grown-ups looked at all the evidence. They found out that he had indeed done things that were not honest. He was 'convicted,' which means the court agreed that he was guilty of corruption. It's like when your teacher finds out someone cheated on a test. The teacher listens and then decides that the person did cheat. The court is doing the same thing, but for much bigger problems in the country.

A Very Long Time for Doing Wrong

The judge decided that because of what Mr. Mamman did, he would be 'sentenced' to 75 years. Wow! That's a super, super long time! It's more than most people live. It means he would have to go to a special place for a very long, long time, as a punishment for what he did. Seventy-five years is like having 75 birthdays! Imagine waiting that many birthdays. It shows how serious it is when someone in an important job is not honest with the country's resources. This kind of punishment sends a strong message. It tells everyone that being honest and fair is very, very important, especially when you are in a position to help many people. **Did you know?** Courts and judges are very important for keeping our countries safe and fair! They make sure everyone follows the rules and that bad actions have consequences.

Where is He Now?

This part of the story is a bit like a mystery! Even though the court said Mr. Mamman was guilty and gave him a sentence, nobody knows exactly where he is right now. The grown-ups say his 'whereabouts are unknown'. This means he is not where he is supposed to be, and the police and other important people are looking for him. They want to make sure that the court's decision is followed. It's important that everyone, no matter how important they are, follows the law.

Why This News Matters to YOU!

You might be thinking, "Why should I, a Class 2 student, care about this?" Well, my young friends, this news matters a lot! When leaders are honest and fair, it helps everyone. If the money meant for electricity is used correctly, then you will have lights to read your books, fans to keep cool, and charging ports for your devices. If the money for roads is used well, you can travel safely. If money for schools is used honestly, you get better classrooms and more books. This story shows us that even big, powerful people must follow the rules. And it reminds us that many good people are working hard to make sure our African countries are fair and strong for everyone, especially for children like you.

Let's All Be Fair and Honest!

So, what can we learn from this? We can learn that being honest is always the best way. Share your toys fairly with your friends. Always tell the truth, even if it's a little bit hard sometimes. And always try to do the right thing. When we all try to be fair and honest, we help make our families, our schools, and our whole country a better place. And that means a brighter future for all of us, with more lights, better schools, and happier children! Thank you for listening, super friends! Until next time, keep shining bright!
summary: A former Nigerian minister, Saleh Mamman, was found guilty of corruption for misusing money meant for electricity, and although he was sentenced to 75 years, authorities do not know where he is.
whyMatters: This story matters because it teaches us that honesty and fairness are very important, especially for people in big jobs who help build good schools, roads, and make sure everyone has electricity, so when leaders are corrupt, it hurts everyone, especially children.

originalTitle: 'They shot my neighbour in the head' - the lakeside city traumatised by war
title: News for Kids: Hope for Uvira!
content:
Hello Little Friends!

Hello, hello, my wonderful friends all across Africa! I'm your friendly news reporter, here to share important stories with you. Today, we're going to talk about a city called Uvira. It's a place where some grown-ups were fighting, but many good people are helping to make things better. It's important to understand, so we can send our love and hope to the children there.

Where is Uvira?

Uvira is a city in a big, beautiful country called the Democratic Republic of Congo. We often call it DR Congo for short. It's right here in our amazing continent of Africa, just like where you live! Uvira is a very special city because it sits right next to a super-duper big lake called Lake Tanganyika. Imagine living next to such a huge body of water where you can see boats and maybe even play by the shore! Did you know? Lake Tanganyika is one of the longest and deepest freshwater lakes in the whole wide world! It's so long that it touches not just DR Congo, but also three other countries. Freshwater is the kind of water we drink, not salty like the ocean, so it's very important for people and animals.

What Happened in Uvira?

Last year, around the time of December, something sad and scary happened in Uvira. Some grown-ups there started fighting with each other. When grown-ups fight in a very big and serious way, and use weapons, we call it a 'war'. This is not a good thing at all, and it makes many people very, very scared. Simple explanation: War is when groups of people, often grown-ups, fight with guns and other tools to hurt each other because they disagree very, very strongly about something important. It is a very sad time, and it makes people feel unsafe in their homes and cities. It's the opposite of being peaceful and playing nicely together.

Why Do Grown-Ups Fight?

Sometimes, grown-ups don't agree on important things. Maybe they disagree about who should be in charge, or how things in their country should be shared, or even how they think people should live. Instead of talking nicely to each other and trying to find a solution that everyone can agree on, sometimes they let their disagreements get too big. When disagreements get too big and people can't talk anymore, sometimes they start to fight. This is never the best way to solve a problem. It makes everyone, especially children, feel very sad and scared. It's a bit like when friends argue, but much, much bigger and more serious. Did you know? It's always better to talk about your problems and disagreements with a grown-up you trust, like a parent or a teacher, instead of fighting! Talking helps us understand each other better.

Scary Times for Children

Imagine not being able to play outside with your friends, or not being able to go to school to learn and draw pictures. That's how it was for many children in Uvira when the fighting started. They and their families felt very scared. Sometimes they had to hide to stay safe from the fighting. This kind of worry and fear makes anyone feel sad inside, especially little ones. Simple explanation: Being scared is a normal feeling when bad things happen around you. It might make your heart beat fast, or your tummy feel a little funny. It's okay to feel scared, but it's important to remember that you are loved, and there are always people who want to keep you safe and make you feel better. All children, everywhere in the world, have the right to feel safe. This is a very important rule, like a promise, that grown-ups have made to children.

Who Helps When Things Are Hard?

When there is trouble, many good and kind people come to help. These helpers are from all over the world, and they work very hard to bring food, clean water, blankets, and even safe places for families to stay. They are like superheroes, but they don't wear capes! They wear kind smiles and have very helping hands. They want to make sure that the children and families in Uvira can be safe and have what they need. Did you know? These special helpers are often called "humanitarian aid workers." They travel to places where people are having a hard time to offer their help and kindness. They are very brave and caring people!

Why is Peace Important?

Peace is a wonderful feeling! Peace means that everyone is safe, happy, and can live together without fighting. When there is peace, children can go to school every day to learn amazing new things. They can play with their friends outside, laugh loudly, and feel calm and joyful. Peace means families can be together and grow strong and happy. Simple explanation: Peace is like a beautiful, sunny day after a big, scary storm has passed. Everything feels calm, warm, and bright again. It means everyone can smile, feel good inside their hearts, and build a happy future together. It’s what everyone wishes for. Did you know? The symbol for peace is often a dove, a beautiful white bird. It flies freely and represents calm and hope.

Hopes for Uvira

We hope that all the children and families in Uvira can feel safe and happy very, very soon. We hope they can go back to school, play their favorite games, and just be kids, full of fun and laughter. Many good people are working very, very hard to make sure peace comes to Uvira and stays there for a long, long time. We believe that with kindness and hard work, things will get much better. Simple explanation: When we all work together, even small steps and kind actions can lead to very big and wonderful changes for the better. It takes time, but hope is a powerful thing!

Let's Send Our Love!

Let's all send our kind thoughts, our warm wishes, and our biggest hopes for peace to the children and families in Uvira. Remember, even when things are tough and scary, there is always hope for a brighter and more peaceful tomorrow. We are all part of one big African family, and we care about each other! Bye for now, friends, stay safe and keep smiling!
summary: This news report tells children about the city of Uvira in DR Congo, where some grown-ups were fighting, making people scared, but many helpers are now working to bring peace and safety back to the children there.
whyMatters: This story matters because it helps us understand that even when bad things happen in the world, people work together to help. It reminds us that all children deserve to be safe and happy, and that we should always hope and work for peace in our communities and around the world.

originalTitle: How Xi-Trump summit failed to yield Iran war breakthrough
title: Big Leaders Talk About Our Ocean Roads!
content:
Hello, My Little News Buddies!

Hello everyone, from your friendly news reporter for Africa! Today we have a very important story to share with you. It's about some big leaders from far-away countries. They had a very special meeting, which we can call a 'big talk'. They talked about keeping our world safe and peaceful. Isn't that wonderful? It's a bit like when you and your friends talk about sharing toys. Sometimes it's easy to agree and play nicely. Other times, it takes a little more talking to find a way that makes everyone happy. That's what happened with these big leaders too.

Who Are These Important Leaders?

Our story today is about two very important leaders. One leader is from a big country called China. His name is President Xi. He is like the head teacher of a very, very big school, but for a whole country! China is a huge country far away, across the seas. The other leaders are from a country called America. America is also a very big country. They have important people who work for their country. These people want to make sure the world is a safe place for everyone. They want peace, just like we all do.

A Special Water Road: The Hormuz Strait

Now, imagine a very busy road. But this road is not made of tarmac or sand. It is a road made of water! It's like a big water highway for ships. This special water road has a long name. It's called the Hormuz Strait. Can you say that with me? Hor-muz Strait! This water highway is very, very important. Lots and lots of big ships travel through it every single day. These ships carry important things from one country to another. It's like a bridge that connects many places.

What Do Ships Carry Here?

Do you know what one of the most important things these big ships carry is? It's oil! Oil is like a special liquid fuel. It helps cars drive very fast. It helps aeroplanes fly high up in the blue sky. It even helps to make electricity for our lights and televisions at home and school. Many countries around the world need this oil. They use it every day to power so many things. So, if ships cannot travel safely through this special water road, the Hormuz Strait, it can be a big problem for many people and many countries.

Did You Know?

Did you know that oil is found deep under the ground and also deep under the sea? People drill very deep to find it. It is a very precious natural resource. It helps power many of the things we use in our daily lives, like cooking our food and charging our phones!

Why is This Water Road Important for Africa?

You might be thinking, "Why does this matter to us in Africa?" Well, it matters a lot! Many countries in Africa also use oil. We need it for our cars, our buses, and sometimes for power in our homes and schools. We need it to help our businesses grow and transport food. If ships cannot bring oil safely or easily, then the oil can become more expensive. This means things might cost more in our shops. Or it might be harder to get fuel for our transport, like getting to school. So, keeping the Hormuz Strait safe helps us here in Africa too! We all want things to be affordable and easy to get.

What Was the Problem?

Sometimes, there have been some worries around this important Hormuz Strait. Some people were a little scared that it might not be safe for ships to pass through. It wasn't completely 'blocked' like a road with a broken-down car. But there were worries that things could go wrong, like accidents or arguments. When people worry about safety, they try to find ways to make things safe again. That's what the big leaders were trying to do during their talk. They wanted to make sure everyone could use this important water road without fear.

America Asked China for Help

The leaders from America had an idea. They went to President Xi of China. They gently 'nudged' him. This means they asked him kindly and encouraged him to help. They wanted China to do 'more' to make the Hormuz Strait safe for all the ships. Why China? Because China is a very big and powerful country. They are friends with many other countries. So, America thought that China could help talk to everyone. They hoped China could help make sure the water road stayed open and safe for all the ships carrying important goods.

Did You Know?

Did you know that China is home to over a billion people? That's more people than in almost any other country in the world! It's a truly giant place with a very long history and lots of beautiful places.

What Did President Xi Say?

When America asked China for help, President Xi listened very carefully. He heard everything they said. But it seems he 'didn't budge' right away. This means he didn't immediately say yes to exactly what America asked for. He didn't change his mind or agree to their specific plan right away. Maybe he wanted to think about it more. Maybe he had different ideas about how to solve the problem. Sometimes, big decisions for a whole country take a lot of time and thought. Leaders need to think about what is best for their own country and also for the rest of the world.

Why is Talking Important?

Even though President Xi didn't immediately agree, it's still very good that they talked. Talking is always the very first step to solving problems. When people talk, they share their ideas and their worries. They listen to each other's thoughts. It helps countries understand each other better, like when you understand your friends better when you chat. Even if they don't agree on everything right away, talking helps stop big fights. It helps keep the peace, which is the most important thing for everyone.

Did You Know?

Did you know that the word 'diplomacy' means when countries talk to each other to solve problems peacefully? It's like being a good friend and talking things out, instead of getting angry or fighting. Diplomats are like super-friendly messengers!

What Happens Next?

So, what happens now? The leaders will probably keep talking. They will keep trying to find a way to make the Hormuz Strait safe for everyone. They all want peace and safety. They all want ships to carry oil and other goods safely across the water. It's like when you try to solve a puzzle. Sometimes you don't get it right away. But you keep trying and trying. And eventually, you find the solution! We hope these leaders will find their solution soon.

Working Together for a Peaceful World

This story reminds us that even big leaders face challenges. But the most important thing is that they keep trying to work together. They want to make sure that our world is a good place. A place where everyone can get what they need. A place where our water roads are safe for all ships to travel. Let's all hope that these leaders will find a way to keep the Hormuz Strait safe and peaceful. This will help us all, even here in our beautiful Africa! Keep shining, my little news friends, and remember to always talk things out!
summary: Important leaders from America and China talked about keeping a special sea road, called the Hormuz Strait, safe for ships, but they still need to find a way to agree on how to help.
whyMatters: When leaders talk about keeping our oceans safe, it helps make sure we have fuel for cars and good things for our homes and schools here in Africa and all over the world.

originalTitle: Xi praises US ‘milestone’ visit and relationship, offers Trump roses
title: Xi praises US ‘milestone’ visit and relationship, offers Trump roses
content: Xi praised the new 'constructive and strategic' relationship with the US, before Trump concluded his China trip.

originalTitle: US seeks indictment of former Cuban leader Raul Castro
title: Big Questions About Helping Planes from Long Ago!
content:
Hello, Super Friends from Africa!

Hello, hello, hello, my wonderful young friends across Africa! It’s your favourite kid-friendly news reporter here, ready to share a story from a place far, far away. Today, we're talking about a big puzzle from a long time ago, and how people are still trying to find answers to make things fair and right. It’s like when something happens in the playground, and the teachers ask questions to understand everything and help everyone be friends again.

Who is Raul Castro?

Imagine your school principal, but for a whole country! That's what a leader is. A very, very long time ago, when your parents were probably just little kids, there was a man named Raul Castro. He was a leader in a country called Cuba. Cuba is a beautiful island country, surrounded by bright blue ocean, very different from many parts of our amazing Africa, but full of wonderful people, just like you! Did you know? Cuba is famous for its lively music and its old, colourful cars that look like they're from a storybook!

A Sad Day Many Years Ago

Now, let's go back to that very long time ago. Something really sad happened. Some small planes were flying in the sky. These weren't just any planes; they were special planes that were trying to help people! But then, oh dear, these helping planes were stopped in the sky in a very serious way. This made many, many people very sad and worried across the world. It’s a bit like someone stopping a friendly messenger from delivering a kind message.

What are 'Humanitarian Planes'?

'Humanitarian' is a big, grown-up word, but it just means 'helping people'! So, 'humanitarian planes' are like flying angels that carry important things to help people who are in need. They might carry yummy food for hungry children, special medicine for people who are sick, or warm blankets for those who are cold. Sometimes they even carry toys or books to make kids smile when they are having a tough time. Just like how we in Africa often help our neighbours and friends when they need it, these planes were trying to bring kindness and help to people who needed it. It's a beautiful way for people from different places to show they care. Did you know? Many planes fly all around the world every day, carrying important things like letters, gifts, and even fresh fruits from one country to another, connecting us all!

Why Are People Asking Questions Now?

Even though this happened so many years ago, people never forgot about those sad planes. The United States, which is another big country not too far from Cuba, wants to ask lots of questions now. They want to understand exactly what happened that day and why those helping planes were stopped. It’s like if a toy went missing at school, and you need to ask everyone to find out what happened so you can get it back or understand why it’s gone. They want to find out who was in charge and if the rules of kindness and safety were followed. It’s all about making things right and fair, even when a long, long time has passed. This is called seeking 'justice', which means making sure everyone is treated fairly and that truth comes out.

What Does 'Asking Big Questions' Mean?

When grown-ups talk about 'seeking indictment' for a leader, it means they are officially asking that person to come and explain what happened in front of important people, like judges. It’s not saying someone is definitely guilty right away, but it means they need to come and answer questions and share their side of the story. It's a way for big countries to figure out what went wrong and to try to prevent sad things from happening again. It’s like if you had a school rule, and someone broke it, the principal would ask them to explain what happened. This is a very serious way to try and understand the past and learn from it. It’s about being accountable for actions, which means taking responsibility for what you do.

Why This Matters to Us in Africa

Even though this happened far away from our beautiful African continent, this story teaches us something very important. It teaches us about peace. It teaches us about being kind to each other, and how important it is for countries to be friends and help each other. When people help each other, like with those humanitarian planes, it makes the whole world a better, happier place. And when things go wrong, it's important to ask questions and try to make things fair so that sad things don't happen again. We all want a world where everyone can help each other without fear. We want a world where leaders try their best to keep everyone safe and happy, and where problems are solved peacefully. Did you know? Africa is home to many amazing animals like elephants, giraffes, and lions! And people from all over the world come to visit our beautiful continent to see them and experience our vibrant cultures.

Let's Learn from the Past!

So, this news is about grown-ups trying to solve a big puzzle from the past. It’s a reminder for all of us, big and small, to always choose kindness, to help our neighbours and friends, and to try to understand each other. When countries talk nicely and help each other, it builds a happier, safer world for everyone, everywhere! Let's all promise to always be helpers and friends. That’s all for today from your African reporter! Stay kind and curious!

title: Xi takes Trump on tour of Communist Party's seat of power as China visit ends
content: Trump and Xi toured Zhongnanhai, the heavily guarded compound where China's top leaders live and work.

originalTitle: New outbreak of Ebola kills 65 in eastern DR Congo
title: Helping Friends in Congo: Learning About Ebola and Staying Safe!
content:
Hello, Super Reporters!

Hi everyone! Welcome to our special news report from our amazing continent, Africa! Today, we're going to talk about something important happening in a country called the Democratic Republic of Congo. It's a very big country in Africa, just like where we live. Sometimes, people there get sick with something called Ebola. It's important for us to learn about it, so we know how to stay safe and understand how people are helping.

What is Ebola?

Ebola is a sickness that is caused by tiny, tiny germs. These germs are so small you can't see them with your eyes! We call these tiny germs 'viruses'. Imagine something smaller than a grain of sand, that's how small they are! When these tiny germs get inside a person's body, they can make them very, very sick. It's a bit like when you get a cold or the flu, but Ebola can be much more serious. Did you know? Viruses are everywhere, but most of them don't make us sick! Our bodies are very good at fighting off most germs.

How Does Ebola Make People Sick?

When someone has Ebola, they might start to feel very tired. They can get a high fever, which means their body feels very hot. It's like when you have a temperature. They might also have a tummy ache, feel sick, or have a headache. These are all ways our bodies tell us that something is wrong. Because of these symptoms, people with Ebola need a lot of special care from doctors and nurses. Did you know? Doctors and nurses are like real-life superheroes! They work so hard to help people feel better when they are sick.

How Does Ebola Spread?

Ebola can spread from one person to another. But don't worry, it's not like a normal cold that spreads when someone sneezes near you! Ebola spreads if you touch someone who is very, very sick with it. Or, it can spread if you touch things that a very sick person has touched, like their clothes, blankets, or even if you touch their body fluids. That's why doctors and nurses wear special suits to protect themselves when they are helping sick people. They have to be super careful! Did you know? Washing your hands often with soap and water is one of the best ways to keep many kinds of germs from spreading! Try washing your hands for as long as it takes to sing 'Happy Birthday' twice!

Many People Are Sick in Congo

In the eastern part of the Democratic Republic of Congo, some people have gotten sick with Ebola. We heard that about 246 people have been reported as 'cases'. This means 246 people are sick with Ebola and need help. This is called an 'outbreak' because many people are getting sick in the same place at the same time. When there's an outbreak, everyone works extra hard to stop the sickness from spreading more.

Sad News for Some Families

Sadly, we also heard that 65 people became so sick that their bodies couldn't fight the tiny germs anymore, and they passed away. This is very sad news for their families and for the whole community. When someone dies, it means their body stops working, and they are no longer with us. Our thoughts are with everyone who is sad in Congo right now.

Heroes Are Helping!

But there is good news too! There are many brave doctors, nurses, and health workers who are in Congo right now, working very hard to help. They are like real-life heroes who put on special protective clothes to care for the sick people. They give them medicine, make sure they are comfortable, and try their best to help them get better. They are trying to stop the tiny germs from spreading to more people. Did you know? The Democratic Republic of Congo is the second-largest country in Africa by land area! It's home to many wonderful people and amazing animals like gorillas and okapi.

Africa's Special Health Team

There's a very important group called Africa's top health agency. Think of them as the best doctors and health experts for all of Africa! They are helping Congo by sending even more doctors, medicines, and good advice. They are working together to make sure that the people in Congo get the best help possible. They want to protect everyone and help stop the sickness. Did you know? This health agency helps many countries in Africa work together to fight against different sicknesses and keep people healthy! They are like a big team for our whole continent.

What Can We Do?

Even though we might be far away, we can still do helpful things. We can be kind and thoughtful. We can learn about different countries and people in Africa, like our friends in Congo. And most importantly, we can all remember to keep our hands clean by washing them with soap and water often. This helps protect us from many types of germs and keeps us healthy, just like the doctors tell us!

A Message of Hope

It's important to remember that there are many smart and brave people working every day to make the world a healthier place. With their help, and by everyone working together, we can hope that the people in Congo will get better, and the sickness will stop spreading. Let's send our kind thoughts to all the children and families in the Democratic Republic of Congo!
summary: An outbreak of a sickness called Ebola has affected 246 people and sadly taken the lives of 65 in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo, but brave doctors and Africa's health experts are working hard to help.
whyMatters: This matters because Ebola is a serious sickness, and it's important to know that doctors and special health teams are working very hard to help the sick people in Congo and stop the germs from spreading, so everyone can stay safe and healthy.

originalTitle: Coleman to end 17-year Everton playing career
title: Seamus Coleman: A Football Star's New Adventure!
content:
Hello, Amazing Kids!

Hello, hello, my wonderful friends all over Africa! I'm your friendly news reporter, and I have some exciting news from the world of football! Do you love watching football? Do you love kicking a ball with your friends? Well, get ready to hear about a very special football player.

His name is Seamus Coleman. Can you say that? SEE-mus COLE-man. He is a super strong and very kind man who plays football. He has been playing for a big team called Everton. But guess what? He is going to finish playing for Everton very soon!

It's a bit like when you finish one class at school and get ready for a new one. It's a big change!

Who is Seamus Coleman?

Seamus Coleman is a fantastic football player from a country called Ireland. He plays on the right side of the field. This means he runs up and down, helping his team score goals and stopping other teams from scoring. He is very fast and very brave!

He is also a "captain." Did you know what a captain is? A captain is like the leader of the team. He helps his teammates, talks to the referee, and shows everyone how to play fair and strong. Seamus was a very, very good captain. Everyone on his team looked up to him.

Imagine being the captain of your school's football team! That's how important Seamus was for Everton.

Did you know? Football is played with a round ball! You can't use your hands, only your feet, head, and chest, unless you are the goalkeeper!

What is Everton?

Everton is a very old and famous football team. They play in a country called England. Their team colours are blue and white. Many, many people around the world love Everton and watch their games. They play in a big stadium with lots of seats, where fans shout and cheer for them.

A football "club" is just another name for a team. So, Everton Football Club is the team Seamus played for. They have a long history of playing great football and winning many games.

Did you know? In some places, football is called "soccer"! But most of the world calls it football, because you play it with your feet!

17 Years! That's a Long Time!

Now, this is the really amazing part! Seamus Coleman has played for Everton for 17 whole years! Can you believe that? 17 years!

Think about how old you are now. Maybe you are 7 or 8 years old? Seamus has played for Everton for longer than you have been alive! He started playing for Everton when he was a young man, and he grew up with the team.

Playing for one team for so long means he loved the team very much. He saw many players come and go, but Seamus always stayed. He was like a big brother to many new players who joined the team.

Did you know? A football game has two halves, each 45 minutes long, plus a little break in the middle!

Why is Seamus Leaving Everton?

Seamus is leaving Everton because his "contract" is finishing. What is a contract? It's like a special promise paper. This paper says how long a player will play for a team, and how much they will get paid. It's like a special agreement.

So, Seamus's contract with Everton is ending at the end of the "season." A season in football is like a school year. It starts in August and finishes around May or June. When the season ends, his promise paper with Everton will be over.

It's not because he did anything wrong. It's just how football works sometimes. After 17 years, it's time for a change for Seamus and for Everton.

Did you know? There are 11 players on each team on the pitch at one time during a football game!

What Will Seamus Do Next?

We don't know exactly what Seamus will do next. Maybe he will play for a new team! Or maybe he will become a coach and teach other young players how to be amazing footballers. He might even go back to his home in Ireland and spend more time with his family.

Whatever he chooses to do, we know he will do it with all his heart, just like he played for Everton. He is a true hero for all the young kids who dream of playing football one day.

Think about what you want to be when you grow up! Maybe a doctor, a teacher, or a football star like Seamus!

Thank You, Seamus!

The fans of Everton are a little bit sad that Seamus is leaving. But they are also very, very thankful. They will clap and cheer for him one last time. They will remember all the amazing games he played and all the brave things he did on the field.

It's important to say thank you to people who work hard and do good things for a long time. Seamus Coleman showed everyone what it means to be loyal and dedicated.

Did you know? The fans are the people who watch the games and cheer for their favourite team! They are very important!

Football Fun for Kids in Africa!

Here in Africa, football is so much fun! Many of you play football every day with your friends, maybe in a field or even on the street. You might not have a big stadium or fancy boots, but you have a ball and lots of energy!

Just like Seamus, you can learn to be a great team player. You can run fast, kick strong, and help your friends. Most importantly, you can have fun and make new friends while playing this wonderful game.

Who knows, maybe one day, one of you will become a big football star just like Seamus Coleman and play for a famous team! Keep dreaming big, keep playing, and keep smiling!

A Fond Farewell and New Beginnings

So, Seamus Coleman is saying goodbye to Everton, but it's not a sad goodbye forever. It's a goodbye that comes with lots of happy memories and excitement for what comes next. It’s about new adventures!

We wish Seamus all the best in his new journey, and we know he will continue to be a champion, no matter what he does. And for all of you, keep playing football and enjoying the beautiful game!

This is your kid-friendly news reporter signing off. Stay curious and keep learning! Bye for now!
summary: Seamus Coleman, a kind and strong football player, is leaving his team, Everton, after playing with them for a very, very long time – 17 years!
whyMatters: This is important because Seamus was a big part of the Everton team, like a leader. He showed everyone how to be a good player and friend for many years.

originalTitle: Beckham becomes UK's first billionaire sportsman
title: Wow! Famous Footballer David Beckham Has So Much Money!
content:
Hello, my amazing friends in Africa! It's your friendly news reporter here, bringing you super exciting news! Today, we are talking about a very famous football player. His name is Sir David Beckham. He used to kick a football like magic, and now something even more amazing has happened to him! He has become a 'billionaire'! Let's find out what that means.

Who is Sir David Beckham?

Sir David Beckham is a very famous man from a country far away called the United Kingdom, or UK for short. Many years ago, he was a superstar football player. He played for big teams, like Manchester United and Real Madrid, and he even played for his whole country, England! He was the captain of the England team, which means he was like the leader on the field, telling everyone what to do. He was known for his amazing kicks, especially when he took free kicks – whoosh! Straight into the goal! Did you know? David Beckham started playing football when he was a very small boy, just like many of you might be doing right now with your friends! He loved the game with all his heart, and he practiced a lot to become very good.

What is a 'Billionaire'?

Now, let's talk about this big word: 'billionaire'. Do you know what a billionaire is? It's someone who has a LOT, LOT, LOT of money! Imagine a huge pile of coins, so tall it reaches the clouds! That's how much money a billionaire has. It's more money than you can count on all your fingers and toes, and all your friends' fingers and toes too! It means they have 1,000 million of something. That's a super, super big number that is hard to even imagine! If you had one billion sweets, you could give every single person in a big country like Nigeria a sweet, and still have many, many left over! It's an almost unbelievable amount of money. David Beckham now has this huge amount of money, which is wonderful news for him!

How Did He Earn So Much Money?

You might think, 'He played football, so he got money from that!' And you would be absolutely right! He earned a lot of money when he was a brilliant football player because he was one of the best in the world. People loved watching him play, and big teams paid him well to kick the ball for them. But guess what? He kept working super hard and being very smart even after he stopped playing football! That's how he got even more money. After he stopped playing, he worked with many big companies that make cool clothes, shoes, and even watches. He helped them show off their amazing stuff. People saw David Beckham wearing something, and they wanted it too! This is called 'advertising' or 'endorsement'. He also helped to start new businesses and made smart choices with his money, investing it wisely so it could grow. Did you know? David Beckham even has his own football team now! It's called Inter Miami and they play in America. So, even though he doesn't play on the field anymore, he is still very much a part of the beautiful game of football! So, it wasn't just kicking the ball, but also being very clever with his ideas, helping many different companies, and working hard for many, many years, even after his amazing football playing days were over. He used his fame to do good business.

The First Ever Super Rich Sportsman!

This is the really big news, my friends! David Beckham is now the FIRST ever sportsman from the United Kingdom to become a billionaire! That means no other person who played sports from his country has ever had so much money. It's like he won a super special trophy for being so smart and working so hard for such a long time! This is a very special thing because it shows that if you love what you do, and you keep working hard and smart, even after you stop doing the main thing, you can achieve amazing things. His hard work on the pitch and his smart work off the pitch have paid off wonderfully. Did you know? David Beckham is not just rich, but he also does a lot of good things with his money and fame, like helping children in need through different charities. He uses his voice to make the world a better place.

Football Dreams in Africa

Here in Africa, we love football so much, don't we? Many of you play football with your friends every day. Maybe you dream of being a famous footballer like David Beckham one day, kicking amazing goals and making your country proud. It's good to dream big! David Beckham's story shows us that even if you start by just loving a game, that love and your hard work can grow into something very, very big. It's not just about playing, but also about being smart, being kind, and working hard in everything you do, whether it's at school or on the field. Did you know? Many famous football players from Africa, like Didier Drogba, Samuel Eto'o, and George Weah, also started playing football barefoot in small villages or on dusty fields, just like some of you! They worked incredibly hard and became big stars, inspiring millions.

What Can We Learn from Beckham?

So, what can we learn from Sir David Beckham's amazing story, my little champions? Here are some simple lessons: 1. **Love what you do!** He loved football with all his heart. 2. **Work super hard!** He always gave his very best in every game and every training session. 3. **Be smart with your choices!** He thought carefully about his future and made good decisions. 4. **Keep learning and trying new things!** He didn't just stop after football; he tried new businesses. Even if you don't become a billionaire, you can still have a happy and successful life by following these simple rules. Maybe you want to be a great teacher, or a kind doctor, or a brave farmer. Whatever your dream is, work hard for it with all your heart, just like David Beckham did! So, there you have it, my little champions! Sir David Beckham, the super footballer, is now also a super-duper rich man, a billionaire! He shows us that dreams, hard work, and smart choices can lead to amazing places. Keep kicking those balls and chasing your dreams! Until next time, stay happy and keep shining!
summary: Famous football legend Sir David Beckham has become the first sportsman from the UK to reach an amazing amount of money, making him a billionaire!
whyMatters: This news is important because it shows us that with a lot of hard work, smart choices, and passion, you can achieve amazing things and be very successful, even after you finish playing your main sport, just like David Beckham did.

originalTitle: The making of the £60K bargain who became an Everton legend
title: The making of the £60K bargain who became an Everton legend
content: As Seamus Coleman announces he will leave Everton this summer, BBC Sport looks at the rise of the £60,000 signing who became a club legend.
